landing.seoDescription: DineCue helps you decide where to eat by matching restaurants to your mood, location, preferences, and dining context.
landing.heroBody: Tell DineCue what kind of meal you want. It finds places that actually feel right for you.

sections.howTitle: A better dining decision starts with the whole picture.
sections.howBody: DineCue weighs the signals people actually use when choosing a table.
sections.featuresBody: Distance matters, but it is not the whole decision. DineCue looks for places that match the occasion, appetite and company.
sections.capabilitiesBody: Use DineCue when you need a place, when a menu feels too broad, or when you want to know whether a restaurant fits the moment.
sections.differenceTitle: Not just search. Not just chat.
sections.differenceBody: DineCue is designed for the moment when you need to choose, not just browse. It brings your real context into the dining decision.
sections.plansBody: Start free. Go deeper when you need more.
sections.scenariosTitle: For the nights when filters are not enough.
sections.trustTitle: A dining assistant should feel helpful, not invasive.
sections.trustBody: We only show places DineCue can actually find. When something is uncertain, DineCue keeps the recommendation honest.

features.title: Context before proximity
features.text: A close place is useful only when it fits the meal you actually want.

features.title: Curated, not endless
features.text: DineCue narrows the field so every suggestion has a reason to exist.

features.title: Clear next steps
features.text: See why a place works, what to know, and what to do next.

capabilities.title: Find a place
capabilities.text: Turn a mood, craving, location and context into a short set of places worth choosing.

capabilities.title: Menu scan
capabilities.text: Make a long menu easier to understand when you want clearer choices.

capabilities.title: Restaurant fit check
capabilities.text: See whether a specific place fits the meal you have in mind before you commit.

difference.title: Map search shows places
difference.text: Maps are great when you already know what to look for. But ratings, distance and photos do not always answer the real question: “Does this place fit tonight?”

difference.title: General tools give ideas
difference.text: Broad assistants can suggest possibilities, but dining decisions need real places, current context and careful wording when something is uncertain.

difference.title: DineCue helps decide
difference.text: DineCue starts with your moment, then helps you find places that actually fit your mood, company, budget and appetite.

free.name: Free
free.description: For trying DineCue and making better everyday dining decisions.
features: Find places for your mood and context

pro.name: Pro
pro.description: For people who use DineCue more often while traveling, planning dinners or comparing places.
features: More room for travel and planning

scenarios.title: Family dinner
scenarios.text: Comfortable enough for kids, still good for adults.

scenarios.title: Date night
scenarios.text: Atmosphere, pace and a little sense of occasion.

scenarios.title: Quick lunch
scenarios.text: Efficient, fresh and near where you need to be.

scenarios.title: Quiet evening
scenarios.text: Low noise, warm lighting and space to talk.

scenarios.title: Good-value dinner
scenarios.text: Worth choosing without feeling like a compromise.

examples.prompt: Quiet dinner with warm lighting, seafood, not too formal.
examples.reason: A polished choice for talking over dinner.

examples.prompt: Family dinner where kids can settle in and adults still eat well.
examples.reason: Comfortable pacing and familiar dishes.

examples.prompt: Date night, elegant but not stiff, good wine by the glass.
examples.reason: Feels special without becoming formal.

examples.prompt: Excellent coffee and a quiet corner for reading.
examples.reason: A gentle stop for slow coffee.

examples.prompt: Quick lunch between meetings, fresh and not heavy.
examples.reason: Easy to choose and easy to leave.

examples.prompt: Local food, simple table, no touristy feeling.
examples.reason: A grounded place with regional comfort.

examples.prompt: Seafood dinner near the water with a quiet mood.
examples.reason: Built around clean flavors and soft pacing.

examples.prompt: Vegetarian dinner that still feels generous.
examples.reason: Vegetable-led dishes with real presence.

examples.prompt: Good value dinner that does not feel like a compromise.
examples.reason: Comforting choices with a careful bill.

examples.prompt: Outdoor seating, easy conversation, late golden hour.
examples.reason: A pleasant table for lingering outside.

examples.prompt: Drinks first, maybe small plates if the night opens up.
examples.reason: A flexible start without pressure.

examples.prompt: Dessert after dinner, something cozy and not loud.
examples.reason: A gentle finish to the evening.

examples.prompt: Business dinner, polished service, easy to talk.
examples.reason: Professional without feeling cold.

examples.prompt: Cozy brunch, good eggs, not a queue situation.
examples.reason: Weekend feeling without the chaos.

examples.prompt: Late-night bite, satisfying, safe, still open-feeling.
examples.reason: A solid answer after plans run late.

examples.prompt: Kid-friendly dinner with a little outdoor space.
examples.reason: Room to relax without sacrificing flavor.

examples.prompt: Premium but relaxed, no stiff white-tablecloth mood.
examples.reason: Elevated without the performance.

examples.prompt: Casual Turkish food, warm bread, grilled things, friendly room.
examples.reason: A comfortable table for shared plates.

examples.prompt: Berlin dinner: cozy, vegetarian-friendly, easy by transit.
examples.reason: A city-night choice with relaxed energy.

examples.prompt: London lunch: quick, good value, near the office.
examples.reason: A practical lunch that still feels chosen.

examples.prompt: Rainy evening, soup or noodles, somewhere comforting.
examples.reason: A warming answer for weather and mood.

examples.prompt: Birthday dinner, small group, celebratory but intimate.
examples.reason: Special enough without losing conversation.

examples.prompt: Solo dinner at the counter, excellent food, no awkwardness.
examples.reason: A place where eating solo feels natural.

examples.prompt: Light dinner before a show, close, punctual, delicious.
examples.reason: Good timing without rushing the meal.

auth.seoDescription: Sign in to DineCue with a secure email code.
auth.introTitle: Your next good table starts with a sentence.
auth.introBody: Sign in with a one-time email code and let DineCue help you choose a place that feels right for you.
auth.sent: We sent a code to your email.

find.title: What should dinner feel like?
find.prompt: Tell DineCue what you are looking for. Mood, company, budget, craving, neighborhood.
find.placeholder: Quiet dinner near Karakoy, good seafood, not too formal...

status.title: Preparing your picks
status.body: DineCue is reading your request and shaping a short list you can actually decide from.

recommendations.title: Your best fits
recommendations.oneResultTitle: We found one place that closely matches your request.
recommendations.oneResultHint: Want more options? Try broadening your preferences.
recommendations.loadErrorBody: Please try again in a moment.
recommendations.failedTitle: Something went wrong while preparing your recommendations.
recommendations.failedBody: Try another cue or adjust what you are looking for.

home.title: What kind of table would feel right today?
home.body: Start with mood, context, and location. DineCue turns what you want into a short, useful set of dining picks.

cards.title: Find tonight's fit
cards.text: Start with a sentence and let DineCue prepare three decision-ready picks.

cards.title: Revisit past moods
cards.text: See recent recommendation sessions when you want to return to an idea.

cards.title: Keep places close
cards.text: Saved places appear here when a recommendation feels worth remembering.

cards.title: Menu scan
cards.text: A polished space for the next food-aware workflow.

history.title: Recent dining decisions
history.emptyBody: Once you create sessions, your recent dining ideas will appear here.

saved.title: Places worth remembering
saved.emptyBody: Save a recommendation when a place feels worth keeping.

profile.title: Your DineCue profile
profile.saveError: We couldn’t save your profile. Please try again.
profile.preferencesBody: These details are used to make recommendations more relevant. Defaults and missing choices are labeled clearly.
profile.preferencesIncomplete: Some dining preferences are not set yet.
profile.preferencesComingSoon: A fuller preferences editor is coming next. For now, DineCue will only treat clearly saved choices as preferences.
profile.freePlanBody: Your free plan includes 5 dining decisions each month.
profile.proSoon: Pro with more monthly decisions is coming soon.

placeholders.menuBody: Soon you will be able to turn a menu into clearer choices for your mood, preferences, and table.
placeholders.fitBody: Soon DineCue will help you understand whether a specific place feels right for you before you commit.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.startRecommendation: We couldn’t start your recommendations. Please try again.
errors.unreachable: We couldn’t reach DineCue right now.
errors.validation: Please check the form and try again.
errors.quotaExceeded: You’ve used your free decisions for this month. More will be available when your monthly limit resets.
errors.proComingSoon: Pro with more monthly decisions is coming soon.
